pr.title: Use HSD assert macros for hand-expanded asserts
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Draft PR sweeping hand-expanded __assert/OSReport patterns into HSD_ASSERT, HSD_ASSERTMSG, and HSD_ASSERTREPORT where the filename argument was __FILE__ or the current basename, plus adding an assert-macros checker and style-check workflow. The PR was closed unmerged with reviewer direction to split source refactors from checker integration and fold checks into #2670.

key_files.role: new non-blocking GitHub Actions style-check workflow running tools/test_check.py and tools/check.py --msg-style actions src
added assert-macros check implementation, enabled by default with --no-assert-macros disable flag per PR body
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:adds the assert-macros check to tools/check.py

key_files.role: added checker fixtures for assert macro detection and exemptions
review-comment target; reviewer said tooling must have a singular entrypoint/monolithic tool
converted NULL item assertions with reports to HSD_ASSERTREPORT
converted Yoshi error reports plus asserts to HSD_ASSERTREPORT
converted several fighter OSReport plus assert blocks to HSD_ASSERTREPORT
converted OSReport plus assert to HSD_ASSERTREPORT
converted one assert-report site and left one byte-sensitive __assert with @todo
converted plain and report assertions to HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTREPORT
converted OSReport plus assert to HSD_ASSERTREPORT
converted device overflow report/assert blocks to HSD_ASSERTREPORT
converted repeated dynamics bound checks to HSD_ASSERTREPORT
assert macro cleanup plus local rename to preserve stringified condition per PR body
left expanded assert with @todo because macro form changed object bytes inside inline function per PR body
assert macro cleanup plus local rename to preserve stringified condition per PR body
left expanded assert with @todo because structural change needed first per PR body
left expanded assert with @todo because macro form changed object bytes inside inline function per PR body
assert macro cleanup plus local rename ptr/buf to p to preserve stringified condition per PR body
left expanded assert with @todo because structural change needed first per PR body

what_changed: Converted direct __assert(...) calls to HSD_ASSERT, HSD_ASSERTMSG, or HSD_ASSERTREPORT where the assert filename was __FILE__ or the file's own basename.
Converted OSReport followed by __assert into HSD_ASSERTREPORT at many sites, preserving report strings and formatting arguments in the examples shown.
Kept asserts with different filenames or header-inline provenance out of the macro sweep because HSD assert macros hard-code __FILE__.
Left 5 expanded assert sites annotated with @todo when macro conversion changed object bytes or needed structural work first: ftchangeparam.c, mnhyaku.c, id.c, tobj.c, and plbonuslib.c.
Renamed locals in synth.c and menu files to reproduce original stringified assert conditions: ptr/buf to p, and data/menu to user_data, as stated in the PR body.
Added assert-macros linting to tools/check.py, fixtures to tools/test_check.py, and a non-blocking style-check workflow.
PR was closed without merge; later issue comment requested splitting src changes and integrating checks into #2670.
smart_moves: Limited macro conversion to cases where macro __FILE__ semantics could represent the original filename argument.
Used HSD_ASSERTREPORT when preserving OSReport side effects/message text was necessary.
Reverted conversions that changed object bytes and recorded visible @todo exemptions instead of forcing a non-matching macro form.
Added checker fixtures for edge cases called out in the PR body: __FILE__, own-basename strings, foreign filenames, @todo exemptions, macro-definition regions, and multiline calls.
Kept the new CI workflow non-blocking for lint findings, failing only on internal checker errors.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When replacing hand-expanded assert blocks, choose HSD_ASSERT for plain asserts, HSD_ASSERTMSG when preserving a custom condition string, and HSD_ASSERTREPORT when an OSReport side effect/message must remain.
Do not macro-convert expanded asserts whose filename argument is a different source file or comes from a header-inline expansion unless the resulting __FILE__ semantics are correct.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:Asserts passing a different filename...cannot use the macros

decomp_lessons.lesson: Assert macro cleanup can require preserving exact stringified condition text; local variable names may need to match the original condition string for byte-identical output.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:where the assert string preserves an original local's name
human_pr_text:synth.c ptr/buf -> p; mnmain.c/mndeflicker.c data/menu -> user_data

decomp_lessons.lesson: If an assert macro conversion changes object bytes, leave the expanded form with a specific @todo rather than accepting a regression.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:every conversion that changed bytes was reverted and @todo'd

naming_conventions.convention: For assert macro conversions that rely on #cond stringification, preserve or restore local variable names that appear in the original assert condition string.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:where the assert string preserves an original local's name

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use before/after full build object SHA checks to validate broad mechanical cleanup; PR body claims all 1046 compiled objects were byte-identical and build.sha1 passed.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:python configure.py && ninja before and after

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: When macro expansion changes comparison polarity or inline-function bytes, keep expanded __assert and annotate with @todo for later byte-matching work.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:ftchangeparam.c float-compare polarity; mnhyaku.c, id.c inline functions

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Checker fixtures should cover accepted, rejected, and exemption cases before enabling an automated sweep check.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:fixtures cover __FILE__ form, own-basename form, foreign-filename non-match, @todo exemption, macro-definition regions, and calls split across lines

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: The tooling needs to be monolithic or have a singular entrypoint; a multi-file Python module is acceptable only if it exposes one entrypoint.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: It may make sense to merge the script and refactors in separate PRs.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Close this PR, split out src changes, and integrate the checks into #2670.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use HSD assert macros only when filename semantics match
accepted_candidate_records.text: Direct __assert calls can be converted to HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTMSG/HSD_ASSERTREPORT when the filename argument is __FILE__ or the current file basename. Expanded asserts using a different filename or header-inline provenance should remain expanded unless the macro's __FILE__ behavior is correct.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:Asserts passing a different filename...cannot use the macros

accepted_candidate_records.title: Preserve report side effects with HSD_ASSERTREPORT
accepted_candidate_records.text: When a hand-expanded assertion includes OSReport before __assert, use HSD_ASSERTREPORT so the report message and arguments remain part of the source-level operation.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Revert assert macro conversions that disturb object bytes
accepted_candidate_records.text: For mechanical assert macro sweeps, verify byte identity and leave byte-changing sites expanded with a clear @todo instead of forcing the macro form.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:every conversion that changed bytes was reverted and @todo'd instead

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify filename boundary for HSD assert macro conversion
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding to assert/report macro standards: HSD_ASSERT-family macros are suitable only when their __FILE__ expansion preserves the original assert filename semantics; leave foreign-filename or header-inline asserts expanded unless local evidence identifies an equivalent source-level helper.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:Asserts passing a different filename...cannot use the macros
source_update_candidates.reason: PR gives concrete boundary conditions for when macro cleanup is unsafe.

source_update_candidates.title: Assert macro stringification can make local names matching-relevant
source_update_candidates.text: Consider noting that HSD_ASSERT condition stringification can make local variable names semantically relevant for matching; if the original assert string includes a local name, preserve or restore that local name rather than accepting a mismatched condition string.
source_update_candidates.reason: PR body explicitly describes local renames done to reproduce original stringified assert strings.

source_update_candidates.title: Split broad mechanical source refactors from checker/tooling integration
source_update_candidates.text: Consider documenting review preference that broad source sweeps and new checker/tooling integration should usually be separate PRs, with tooling exposed through a singular entrypoint.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:Might make sense to merge the script and the refactors in separate PRs
text_corpus:split out the src changes, and integrate the checks into #2670
source_update_candidates.reason: Reviewer feedback indicates process/tooling boundaries, but this should be curated as project policy before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not mark this PR's changes as accepted or merged; metadata shows CLOSED with merged_at null and a closing comment directing split-out work.

Do not promote the proposed style-check workflow as accepted CI policy from this PR; reviewer requested check integration into #2670 instead.
